steps.title: Compare the side lengths
steps.content: In triangle $PQR$, the vertical side from $P$ to $Q$ has length $3$, and the horizontal side from $P$ to $R$ has length $3$. In triangle $LMN$, the matching vertical and horizontal side lengths are both $3+k$.

steps.title: Identify matching shapes
steps.content: Both triangles are right isosceles triangles. That means each has one right angle, and its two acute angles are equal.

steps.title: Relate the acute angles
steps.content:
In a right triangle, the two acute angles sum to $90^\circ$. Since the measure of angle $Q$ is $t^\circ$, the other acute angle has measure
$$(90-t)^\circ$$
The corresponding angle in the larger triangle has the same measure. The answer is C.
